title: Analitiq canonical types
description:
Analitiq's profile of the Apache Arrow logical type system. Canonical types are strings that identify an Arrow logical type in the form used by `type_map.canonical` entries on connector definitions and `arrow_type` fields throughout the schema contracts.

Standard (one screen):

Base form: PascalCase Arrow type name from `arrow/format/Schema.fbs`.

Three parameter shapes:
  - Bare name, no params — scalar types: `Utf8`, `Int64`, `Boolean`, `Date32`, `Binary`.
  - Parens `( )` for value params — parameterized scalars (units, precision/scale, byte widths): `Decimal128(38, 9)`, `Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)`, `FixedSizeBinary(16)`.
  - Angle brackets `< >` for type params — nested types (a type inside a type): `List<Int64>`, `Struct<id:Int64, name:Utf8>`, `Map<Utf8, Int64>`.

Unit values inside parens are the literal Flatbuffers enum identifiers — uppercase:
  - `TimeUnit`: `SECOND`, `MILLISECOND`, `MICROSECOND`, `NANOSECOND`.
  - `IntervalUnit`: `YEAR_MONTH`, `DAY_TIME`, `MONTH_DAY_NANO`.

Timezone (Timestamp only): optional second arg. `null`, IANA name, `Etc/GMT±N`, or fixed `±HH:MM` offset.

Full canonical examples:
  Utf8
  Int64
  Boolean
  Date32
  Decimal128(38, 9)
  Decimal256(76, 0)
  Timestamp(MICROSECOND)
  Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)
  Timestamp(MILLISECOND, +05:30)
  Time32(SECOND)
  Time64(NANOSECOND)
  Duration(MICROSECOND)
  Interval(YEAR_MONTH)
  FixedSizeBinary(16)
  List<Int64>
  LargeList<Utf8>
  FixedSizeList<Int64>[8]
  Struct<id:Int64, name:Utf8>
  Map<Utf8, Int64>
  Dictionary<Int32, Utf8>

Why uppercase unit names instead of PyArrow shorthand (`us`/`ms`/`ns`): the uppercase identifiers are the only spelling that's actually in the Arrow specification (the Flatbuffers `TimeUnit` / `IntervalUnit` enums). PyArrow's shortcodes are a Python-library convenience; they have no clean form for `IntervalUnit` and read ambiguously in spec text (`us` parses as 'United States' to a casual reader or LLM). The translation to PyArrow constructor args is the runtime's job, not the spec's.

Why parens for value params but angle brackets for type params: parens hold values (integers, enums); angle brackets hold types. Same convention as generics in most typed languages (Java/Rust/TypeScript/C++).

Reference: https://arrow.apache.org/docs/format/Columnar.html#logical-types.

Scope note: patterns for nested types (List/Struct/Map/Union/Dictionary/RunEndEncoded) validate the outer shape only; deep validation of inner canonical types is the validator API's responsibility, not JSON Schema's.

Version note: Arrow 15+ view types (`Utf8View`, `BinaryView`, `ListView`, `LargeListView`) are not part of this canonical vocabulary. The platform's pinned PyArrow supports them (currently `^20`), so their exclusion is a deliberate vocabulary-scope choice, not a library limitation; add them here if the platform adopts them end-to-end.
$comment: Validating a value directly against this document's URL checks it against the strict canonical_type vocabulary. Type-map regex rules that permit ${name} templates reference the #/$defs/canonical_type_or_template fragment explicitly.

canonical_type.title: canonical_type
canonical_type.description: A canonical type string. Must match one of the Arrow logical type families below.

authored_shape_type.title: Authored-shape JSON container
authored_shape_type.description: Bare authored-shape JSON container markers. `Object` declares a JSON object with a sibling `properties` map describing each child (recursive). `List` declares a JSON array with a sibling `items` field spec describing the element (recursive). `Json` declares an opaque JSON object or array with no inner shape — no `properties` or `items` permitted. Distinct from the parameterized forms `Struct<...>` and `List<...>`, which describe fully-typed nested records. Sibling-key enforcement is performed by the validator API and the Pydantic models in `k2m.models.stream` / `k2m.models.endpoints`, not by this string vocabulary.

null_type.title: Null
null_type.description: Arrow Null logical type. Column contains only null values. Zero storage.

boolean_type.title: Boolean
boolean_type.description: Arrow Boolean logical type. 1-bit values: true or false.

integer_type.title: Int / UInt
integer_type.description: Arrow signed and unsigned integer logical types at fixed widths of 8, 16, 32, or 64 bits. Width should reflect the source system's declared width — do not upcast `Int32` to `Int64` unless the engine requires it.

floating_type.title: FloatingPoint
floating_type.description: Arrow IEEE-754 floating-point logical types. Float16 is half-precision; Float32 is single-precision; Float64 is double-precision.

binary_type.title: Binary
binary_type.description: Arrow binary logical types. `Binary` is variable-length with 32-bit offsets; `LargeBinary` is variable-length with 64-bit offsets; `FixedSizeBinary(byte_width)` is fixed-length.

string_type.title: Utf8
string_type.description: Arrow UTF-8 string logical types. `Utf8` uses 32-bit offsets (capped ~2 GiB total per array); `LargeUtf8` uses 64-bit offsets.

date_type.title: Date
date_type.description: Arrow Date logical types. `Date32` is days since the Unix epoch; `Date64` is milliseconds since the Unix epoch (must be a multiple of 86_400_000).

time_type.title: Time
time_type.description: Arrow time-of-day logical types. `Time32` supports SECOND and MILLISECOND units; `Time64` supports MICROSECOND and NANOSECOND units.

timestamp_type.title: Timestamp
timestamp_type.description: Arrow Timestamp logical type. Unit is one of SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, NANOSECOND. Timezone is optional; when omitted, the type is zone-naive / local. When present, the timezone is either `null` (explicit zone-naive), an IANA zone (`UTC`, `America/New_York`), an `Etc/GMT±N` offset, or a fixed `±HH:MM` offset. When mapping from a source that declares a zoned type (e.g., Postgres `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`), use `UTC` unless there is a source-specific reason otherwise.

duration_type.title: Duration
duration_type.description: Arrow Duration logical type. Elapsed time with unit SECOND, MILLISECOND, MICROSECOND, or NANOSECOND.

interval_type.title: Interval
interval_type.description: Arrow Interval logical type. YEAR_MONTH stores months as int32; DAY_TIME stores days + milliseconds as two int32s; MONTH_DAY_NANO stores months + days + nanoseconds in 16 bytes.

decimal_type.title: Decimal
decimal_type.description: Arrow Decimal128 or Decimal256 logical type. `Decimal128(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-38; `Decimal256(precision, scale)` supports precision 1-76. Scale may be negative but is typically 0 or positive. Use `Decimal128` unless the source precision exceeds 38.

list_type.title: List / LargeList / FixedSizeList
list_type.description: Arrow list logical types. `List<T>` uses 32-bit offsets; `LargeList<T>` uses 64-bit offsets; `FixedSizeList<T>[n]` has fixed list length `n`. `T` is itself a canonical type; inner-type validation is performed by the validator API.

struct_type.title: Struct
struct_type.description: Arrow Struct logical type. Fields are `name:type` pairs separated by commas: `Struct<id:Int64, name:Utf8, created:Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)>`. Field names follow the source field naming; no case normalization is performed.

map_type.title: Map
map_type.description: Arrow Map logical type with key and value types: `Map<Utf8, Int64>`. Arrow maps are logically unordered; engines may sort keys for efficient lookup.

union_type.title: Union
union_type.description: Arrow Union logical type. `SparseUnion<T1, T2, ...>` stores one buffer per child type, full-length; `DenseUnion<T1, T2, ...>` stores child buffers at length equal to each type's usage count, plus an offsets buffer. Use sparse for few types or uniform distribution; dense for many types with skewed distribution.

dictionary_type.title: Dictionary
dictionary_type.description: Arrow Dictionary logical type. `Dictionary<index_type, value_type>` stores integer indices referencing a shared dictionary of values. Use for low-cardinality string/binary columns to reduce storage.

run_end_encoded_type.title: RunEndEncoded
run_end_encoded_type.description: Arrow RunEndEncoded logical type. `RunEndEncoded<run_end_type, values_type>` stores consecutive repeated values as (run_end_index, value) pairs. Efficient for columns with long runs of identical values.

canonical_type_or_template.title: canonical_type_or_template
canonical_type_or_template.description: A canonical type string OR a templated canonical type carrying `${name}` placeholders in parameter positions. Used by type-map regex rules where parameters are substituted from named capture groups (e.g. `Decimal128(${precision}, ${scale})`). Each parameter position accepts either a LITERAL canonical value at its valid range/enum (`38`, `MICROSECOND`, `UTC`) or a `${name}` placeholder; a literal out of range is rejected exactly as the strict vocabulary rejects it, so `Decimal128(999, 0)` and `Time32(NANOSECOND)` do NOT match. A `${name}` placeholder must be a valid identifier (`[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*`), matching the native capture-group naming it resolves from; `${1bad}` / `${ }` do not match. Outside parameter positions, the canonical Arrow base name must appear verbatim — `not an arrow type ${precision}` does not match. Templated branches use `anyOf` (not `oneOf`) because a literal parameterized canonical (e.g. `Timestamp(MICROSECOND, UTC)`) intentionally matches both the strict canonical_type vocabulary AND the templated branch — both readings are correct, and `anyOf` reflects that. Nested types (List/Struct/Map/Union/Dictionary/RunEndEncoded) carry placeholders inside their inner `<...>` positions automatically (the inner type is any canonical type); `FixedSizeList<T>[n]` additionally accepts a placeholder in the fixed-length `[n]` position. This vocabulary mirrors the runtime `_validate_type_map_canonical` shape check plus the placeholder-name rule its sibling validators enforce; a differential parity test pins that alignment.

anyOf.description: Templated Decimal128(precision, scale) — literal precision constrained to 1-38 (as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder; scale is a literal integer or a placeholder.
Templated Decimal256(precision, scale) — literal precision constrained to 1-76 or a `${name}` placeholder; scale is a literal integer or a placeholder.
Templated Timestamp(unit) or Timestamp(unit, tz) with placeholders allowed in unit and/or tz positions.
Templated Time32(unit) — SECOND/MILLISECOND (the only units valid for the 32-bit width, as the strict vocabulary requires) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Time64(unit) — MICROSECOND/NANOSECOND (the only units valid for the 64-bit width) or a `${name}` placeholder.
Templated Duration(unit) with a placeholder in the unit position.
Templated Interval(unit) with a placeholder in the unit position.
Templated FixedSizeBinary(n) with a placeholder in the byte-width position.

Templated FixedSizeList<T>[n] with a placeholder in the fixed-length position. The inner type T already accepts placeholders via the nested `<...>` branch of canonical_type; this branch adds the `[n]` length position (which the strict vocabulary constrains to a positive integer).
